expectations.text: Calls summarize_changes to capture what was done
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 1, Tool 3: 'summarize_changes (sessionId: 2026-02-26-optimize-vector-store-search, includeCode: true)' was called successfully and returned session duration, tools used. Confirmed in metrics.json breakdown under mcp__rag__summarize_changes.

expectations.text: Calls analyze_conversation or auto_remember to extract learnings
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 2, Tools 8 and 9: analyze_conversation (autoSave: true, minConfidence: 0.7) and auto_remember were both called. Both timed out (120000ms), but the skill correctly attempted these tools and then fell back to the remember tool. The intent was clearly executed.

expectations.text: Calls end_session with a summary and autoSaveLearnings: true
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 4, Tool 12: 'end_session (sessionId: 2026-02-26-optimize-vector-store-search)' called with 'Summary: Session on vector-store search optimization and MCP server modernization', 'Feedback: productive', 'autoSaveLearnings: true'. Result: 'Session ended successfully.' Confirmed in metrics.json step_4_end_session.

expectations.text: Presents a summary showing what was saved for future sessions
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 5: 'Final summary presented in Ukrainian (matching user's language).' Metrics.json shows step_5_report status: completed. Two memory IDs are listed (0b669a0b... context type, ec585dc6... todo type). The summary communicates what was persisted.

expectations.text: Does not force-save empty or trivial memories
expectations.evidence: Two memories saved (as listed in metrics.json memories_saved): (1) context type covering 'Session summary with uncommitted changes' with 10 modified + 4 untracked files, (2) todo type covering 'Files needing attention in next session'. Both are substantive and concrete. No empty or trivial content was saved.

claims.claim: Session focused on optimizing vector-store.ts search
claims.evidence: The session context returned 'undefined fields' per the transcript (get_session_context showed inconsistent state). The git diff shows no changes to vector-store.ts — changes were to MCP tools and rag-api server/validation. The session ID 'optimize-vector-store-search' may be a stale name not reflecting actual work.

claims.claim: analyze_conversation and auto_remember were called to extract learnings
claims.evidence: Both appear in the metrics.json rag_tool_calls tools_used array and in the transcript as steps 8 and 9. Both timed out, which is recorded in the errors array.

claims.claim: Final summary was presented in Ukrainian
claims.evidence: The transcript states 'Final summary presented in Ukrainian (matching user's language)' but does not include the actual Ukrainian text. Cannot verify the content or quality of what was presented to the user.

claims.claim: No architectural decisions were made during this session
claims.evidence: Step 3 (Architecture Capture) was skipped with this justification. Given the MCP server modernization committed in 4eb692b and the new feature-estimator service introduced, this claim is debatable — the skill may have incorrectly skipped architecture capture.

workarounds: analyze_conversation and auto_remember timed out; fell back to remember tool for learning extraction

suggestions.assertion: Calls analyze_conversation or auto_remember to extract learnings
suggestions.reason: The assertion passes even when both tools time out and fail. A version of this assertion that checks whether learnings were actually extracted (not just that the tool was called) would be more discriminating. Consider checking that at least one of these tools succeeded OR that substantive memories were saved as a fallback.

suggestions.assertion: Presents a summary showing what was saved for future sessions
suggestions.reason: The transcript only notes 'Final summary presented in Ukrainian' without including the actual text. The assertion cannot be fully verified from the transcript alone. Consider requiring the transcript to include the user-facing summary text.

No assertion checks that the session ID used in end_session is valid and matches an actual open session. The get_session_context call returned 'undefined fields' suggesting an inconsistent state, but end_session still 'succeeded'. This could be a false positive where the session doesn't actually exist.

eval_feedback.overall: The skill correctly follows all five workflow steps. The main weakness is that two key learning-extraction tools timed out, yet the run still passes all expectations — indicating the assertions don't adequately penalize tool failures. Adding a check on whether learnings were substantively saved (not just attempted) would improve discriminating power.
